title: Dead-letter poison events
description: Events that fail processing after bounded attempts move to a dead-letter topic instead of blocking the partition.
body: Use when a single unprocessable event could fail repeatedly on a partition. Do not let a poison event redeliver forever, which on an ordered partition halts every later event. Actions: bound attempts, then publish the failed event with error metadata to a dead-letter topic, commit the offset, and continue; alert and retain enough context to replay after a fix. Validation signals include a test that a permanently failing event reaches the DLT after N attempts and later events on the partition still progress. Risk boundary: no DLT on an ordered partition means one bad event stalls the whole stream; treat as a blocking design gap.
